problem_statement: AI coding agents make implicit architectural decisions (framework, database, auth, deployment, tool orchestration) at scale, speed, and opacity, yet almost no one reviews these as architectural choices, leaving them ungoverned and undocumented.
magnitude_evidence: "The 5.9× code growth (141→827 LoC) and 3× file increase in the case study" produced by prompt wording alone; agents can "scaffold entire projects from a single prompt" while decisions "take seconds, arrive bundled, and leave no record"
insufficiency_evidence: "Agents scaffold systems in minutes; teams need hours or days to audit them"; existing guardrails (hooks, .cursorrules, AGENTS.md) "constrain reactively" and "Proactive guidance that flags consequences before generation is still missing"
solution_mechanisms: treat prompt specs as architectural artifacts in review
pre-generation impact statement flagging added components/LoC/failure modes
complexity thresholds that trigger review when exceeded
conformance checks comparing dependency graph to declared constraints
automated ADR extraction from agent reasoning traces
